title: ulmus effusa var. oblongata
intro: Ulmus effusa var. oblongata, also known as Elm-leaved Wych Elm or Narrow-leaved Wych Elm, is a variety of the Wych Elm tree (Ulmus effusa), native to parts of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Ulmus effusa var. oblongata include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, as well as its use as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from its bark and leaves, which are often used to treat a range of health problems.
